why_detected: Average RMSSD across this session was 12.8ms, below the 20ms threshold associated with reduced parasympathetic activity.
impact: Lower RMSSD reflects reduced vagal (parasympathetic) regulation of the heart, which over time is associated with poorer stress resilience and higher cardiovascular load.
recommendation: Consider paced breathing exercises (e.g. 6 breaths/min) and prioritizing sleep consistency, both of which are reliably shown to raise RMSSD.

why_detected: No biomarker in this session crossed an explainable risk threshold.
impact: Cardiovascular indicators during this session were within expected healthy ranges.
recommendation: Continue current monitoring cadence; no action needed.

why_detected: Mean heart rate during the seated baseline session was 108 bpm, above the 100 bpm resting threshold.
impact: A persistently elevated resting heart rate is an independent marker of cardiovascular strain and reduced cardiorespiratory fitness.
recommendation: Recommend a clinical follow-up to rule out underlying causes (anemia, thyroid, dehydration) and consider a structured aerobic conditioning plan.

why_detected: Stress index (derived from skin conductance) increased by ~1.7 points per 30s window across the session, with no plateau.
impact: Sustained, unresolving sympathetic activation prevents recovery and is linked to elevated blood pressure and arrhythmia risk over repeated exposure.
recommendation: Insert active recovery breaks during sustained cognitive or physical load, and monitor whether this pattern repeats across sessions.
